name: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles Empire City

reviews.review_description:
This game feels dated in a way that’s hard to ignore. It feels like an early gen vr game, closer to something you’d expect from PSVR1 than a modern release. The physics lack any real sense of weight or impact, which undercuts one of the most important parts of a game like this: being a ninja turtle should feel powerful and cool, but here it doesn’t. It feels like kind of a drag.

Combat, the one thing i expected to be nailed flawlessly in a martial arts themed game, is shockingly plain. It feels like the original skyrim vr, where i swing my sword and it connects, but the enemy only has one animation to show this. Hit them in the leg? Their head bounces back. Hit them in the belly? Their head bounces back. This plus the lack of any in depth combat, like, say, being able to punch empty handed, leads the fighting to feel like a chore rather than a reward. 

Encounters are repetitive and lack depth, rarely evolving beyond basic, unengaging brawls and one of a few minigame type thi gs. This extends to the core gameplay loop, which quickly becomes predictable: you’re dropped into a semi-open map (the first two available are almost identical),  sent to a handful of marked locations where you fight a few enemies, then complete one of a small set of mini-games. These mini-games, hitting targets, inputting button sequences, or rotating dials to match patterns, feel like filler meant to pad game time than meaningful gameplay.

What’s most surprising, though, is the lack of personality. The Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles are known for their distinct voices and humor, but here they all sound nearly identical, and the charm you’d expect from the franchise is entirely absent. There’s very little wit, energy, or character driving the experience, which makes everything feel oddly flat.

Visually, the game is an enormous bore. Environments and character models come across as boxy and outdated, that “last-gen” feeling. After two hours, the story is minimal to the point of being forgettable, and the small bits of narrative you do get fail to engage or build momentum.

Even the turtle lair is mostly decorative. While each turtle has their own room and there are props like arcade machines and training equipment, none of it is meaningfully interactive. Outside of leveling up and selecting missions, there’s little reason to spend time there, unless you want to eat pizza off the floor all day. And even the "eating" mechanic is poorly done. I've played cheaper games where characters bite an apple and it shows a bite taken out of the apple, and you can go for more. In this game, any food item just disappears as you bring it to your mouth. It's a total and complete ignorance of the concept of immersion. Imagine my surprise when I find a hat, put it up on my head, then look in a mirror and see thst it is not there. Where did it go? What's the point of having a hat as an item if it doesn't appear on the model?

In addition to all this, I encountered a game breaking bug early on where I was meant to kill 4 enemies, but only 3 ever spawned, locking me into a restart with no other options. How many times is that going to happen?

The game feels like a missed opportunity in many ways. It has the foundation of something that could have been fun, but the lack of polish, personality, and engaging mechanics leaves it feeling dry and outdated. This game is not worth $25. At just under two hours of play time, I've had my fill of it already.

developer_response.body: Thanks for taking the time to share such detailed feedback—we really appreciate it. We’re sorry to hear the experience didn’t meet your expectations. The team is actively working on improvements, including addressing bugs like the one you encountered and some were already patched in with 1.0.2. Your input is valuable and helps us continue to improve the game. Cowabunga 🍕🐢

reviews.review_description: Just wow! We will be spending our nights hunting Quest Achievements with TMNT. The gameplay is extremely fun - climbing and jumping from rooftops to land on the enemies below, helping citizens in the city, using nunchucks in VR 🤯 We appreciate the inclusion of Quest Achievements and look forward to patrolling the city tonight!

developer_response.body: ACHIEVEMENTS INDEED! 👏 Cowabunga, enjoy your time in Empire City!

reviews.review_title: Easily a day one purchase as a TMNT fan!
reviews.review_description: A VR TMNT game has been long overdue for vr! There will be those that will say the visual isn’t the best, but I actually think it’s great cuz it fits the comic style TMNT artwork. Each turtle has their lore accurate unique personalities and weapons, the music matches the vibe, and it’s so fun parkouring on top of the buildings in the city! Will say Michael Angelo’s weapon seems the most OP cuz the combo damage achieved so easily. Highly recommend for any TMNT fan and ofc, any action adventure game fanatics!

One, two, three, four
(Turtles!) Mutant chain reaction
(Turtles!) Livin' underground
(Turtles!) Ninjutsu action
(Turtles!) It's a shell of a town!

Turtles, count it off!
(One) Live by the code of the martial arts
(Two) Never fight unless someone else starts
(Three) Always stick together no matter what
(Four) If all else fails, then it's time to kick butt!

I love bein' a... (I love bein' a...)
I love bein' a Turtle!
Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles
Turtles, count it off!

One, two, three, four
(Turtles!) There's no one better
(Turtles!) Watch out for Shredder!
(Turtles!) They're like no others
(Turtles!) Those teenage brothers!

developer_response.body: Shell yeah! 🐢

reviews.review_description:
Alright, I hate to be that person, but I pre-ordered this a few hours ago, downloaded it, and now it just loads to a completely blank black screen. I’ve tried uninstalling and reinstalling multiple times, but nothing changes.

Has anyone got any advice on how to fix this? I’m really keen to play

developer_response.body: Hey Tim! That doesn't sound right. Let's get that looked at. What device are you playing on? We can also help you more effectively if you wouldn't mind jumping over to our Discord 👉 https://discord.com/invite/cortopia

reviews.review_title: A fan or not, it’s sick!
reviews.review_description: It’s fricking awesome! I haven’t really watched TMNT since I was just a kid, but this game is great! The movement is smooth, fighting feels nice and visuals are pleasing! I definitely recommend this game even if you’re not a huge TMNT fan!;)

Better With Friends, but fun alone as well. Was having a hard time getting into anything solo on VR and this broke my spell. Much needed title if you ask me, even just to beat up dudes. 

Each Turtle feels fairly different but still similar, and you can swap weapons with your teammates! Fire!

reviews.review_title: GO NINJA GO NINJA  INCREDIBLE GAME
reviews.review_description: This game have a great strategy and story mode and collection you find and craft  items you find I played  games of NINJA turtles 🐢  this take it to new level in gaming   love watching series and the movie grow up your team took there most hearts making this game possible many ways  hands down best game ever played worth ever penny awesome game hands down 🤘✔️.....And open world too now that incredible 😲 much support guys 1000 perfect game 🎮

developer_response.body: Booyakasha! Thank you for reviewing! 🐢

reviews.review_description: This game has good graphics. It’s just way too short. The makers needed to add a lot more to the game . if you leveled up each turtle the game gets longer, but I was done at one turtle . when you  beat the game what’s one turtle . ￼then you start back over with the other ones . ￼i’m glad I bought the game, but I’m hoping they add more to it .￼￼￼￼

Combat feels great, blocking. And the abilities are all super fun to combo together.

it's pretty much an open world where you can parkour from building to building.

 I had one complaint about a bug that stopped me from progressing, but the devs are already aware of that and are releasing a patch. It's overall one of the more polished games I've played, also has a pretty intriguing story.

Overall better than i initially thought, has some great replayability too. 


Thanks for the response earlier to my review, and if you don't mind me asking a question, is this game available on quest 2?  I was planning on playing co-op with my buddy but he doesn't have a quest 3. And if it's not, do you guys have any plans to bring it to quest 2?

developer_response.body: Hey there, we're aware of the bug, and a fix will be available tomorrow, Friday. For now, unfortunately, yes, you will need to start on a new save, and either collect all or none of the trash in the tutorial, and then you should be good. We're so sorry for the inconvenience! 🙏

reviews.review_description: alright let me get this straight I got the game for 20$ other people might have gotten it for 25$ but any price is worth it cause HOLY you got a story bosses open world collectables multiplayer crimes it's insane I can't believe this is real definitely one of the best vr games of all time I have been playing vr for 4 years and this is one of the games that make me go back to the old vr days like Batman Arkham Shadow or Zix I hope they update the game cause holy I don't know what to say im out of word's

developer_response.body: We're so glad you're enjoying the game! Cowabunga! 🐢🐢🐢🐢

reviews.review_description: I personally love the game a lot I played it so much and It’s very fun. It might be a little wonky here and there but this game is so AMAZING and it was so worth the price. But a downside is that it’s addictive, but in a very good way. If your a tmnt fan and like vr would recommend. 🐢🐢🐢🐢🐢🐢🐢🐢🐢🍕🍕🍕🍕🍕🍕🍕🍕

I’m using voice to text for this review because, honestly, I feel better spoken verbally when it comes to games like this. I followed the development of this game a bit and got very excited. The early reviews for the demo were off the chains. I got a chance to play it today, and let me tell you, this was made for fans by fans, clearly. The voice acting is perfect. The movement is perfect. The combat is thrilling and silly. Everything feels so polished. Simply put, I cannot wait to jump back in. The weapons between the turtles are extremely different and very fun to tinker with before you decide on which turtle you want to use. The cell shaded art style is beautiful!

My friend is getting this, and we plan to play co-op this weekend. Absolutely amazing job, devs. I will update my review with further thoughts once I finish the game!

developer_response.body: We're so happy you're enjoying the game! Hope you have a great time with your friends (and don't forget to try to steal their weapons ;) )

reviews.review_description: This game let's you interact with almost everything in the layer. It really makes you feel like a ninja turtle. The graphics are a comic style, but that doesn't mean the graphics aren't good. Especially for quest. The game runs really smooth. I pre-ordered the game and it didn't disappoint. If you dont really like ninja turtles you could still enjoy the game. But if you do like ninja turtles get this game Immediately.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for taking the time to review. It means the world to us! 🍕🍕🍕

reviews.review_title: Add a second story with the KRANG
reviews.review_description:
You should add a second story with the KRANG and they try to take over New York with the technodrome and the turtles have to use literally everything they have to stop them, it would be an awesome addition to the absolute perfection that this was, also I’d like to see more dialogue between the characters.  And you should add a last ronin skin for the turtles.

I absolutely love your game and I am a die hard ninja turtles fan and grew up watching the show and playing mutants in manhattan on Xbox:)
Great job👍
I also main Mikey as he’s my favorite character and I can relate to him incredibly well:)

developer_response.body: Thank you for the suggestions, we'll take them home to consider, booyakasha! 🐢

reviews.review_title: Disappointed dnt believe the hype read
reviews.review_description: I had such high hopes for this game being such a huge fan even pre ordered but was sadly disappointed. Combat is eh they could do so much better. You can’t block or anything. You just basically wave ur hands like a mad man and dash a lot. Also it’s soooo boring I’m in total shock with all the hype. All you do is wonder around a ghost city yup completely empty no people or life what’s so ever and by urself can’t even take the crew with you unless you play multiplayer which was all right, but basically the same ￼ . Then you’ll run into some foot soldiers take em out and ya that’s it over and over and over and over ….. literally that’s it!  Also in the trailer they show Michelangelo on the skateboard yet there’s no skateboard that would’ve been cool to at least ride around on in the sewer or in the city but no. The only thing was and when you look in the mirror and see yourself as a turtle, but that only last for five minutes and then you’re over it. ￼I really really wanted this game to be good but now I just want a refund 😔

developer_response.body: Thanks for giving the game a shot and for taking the time to share your feedback, even if it wasn't to your liking. Stay radical 🐢

reviews.review_description:
Very good game and great graphics. It’s unlike any other VR you can not only comeplete the story roam around the entire city and pretty much climb anything.

Fighting can get a tad hard but I think it depends on which turtle you use as well. 

Overall this has been a tremendous experience

developer_response.body: Shell yeah! Thank you so much for taking the time to drop us a review!

reviews.review_description: This game has potential to be a top game on Meta Quest. They just need to add another game mode or something to keep us coming back. You can only play the story mode so many times. The city is great, but add something to it. The crime rate thing isn’t working very repetitive. Maybe boss fight mode where you fight all the bosses in the city multi player. Great job with this game for only $25. Wish it was $50 and had a complete double the content.

The game is great no real complaints except for multiplayer if your gonna buy the game but you don’t have friends that own a vr than it might be a little difficult to play with friends maybe the devs should add ai bots or be able to join random people game  but other than that the parkour  , traversal is amazing its really flaunt as well as the combat if your thinking about picking this up make sure you have friends

As I play further through the game, it becomes more challenging and interesting, and there is definitely a lot to keep you busy. This game is really growing on me and is becoming quite addictive!

I am so happy to finally see the Turtles in VR. Being able to experience an open-world TMNT game with solid voice acting, crafting, and a great overall ambience is fantastic. The gameplay provides a rewarding challenge that keeps me coming back for more.

I’m really enjoying the foundation here and would love to see the experience evolve even further in future updates. I hope to see the graphics refined and a matchmaking system added so we can join sessions with random players rather than just using join codes. It would also be great to have the option to play with the other turtles as a bot team and perhaps add a combat alert for when you're about to be struck from behind. For customization, I'd love to see the skin add-ons evolve into actual model changes rather than just palette swaps, and have those skins reflected on the other turtles in the lair too. While some parts can feel a bit repetitive, I think adding more mission variety would really help round out the experience.
The game has a solid core, and I’d love to see a future expansion pack with more game modes, a versus mode, a cheat toggle menu, and even usable skateboards. I’ll provide an updated review once I have completed it, but for now, I’m having a blast!

reviews.review_title: Good game! Until the fught with bepbop
reviews.review_description:
First of all, LOVE the game, numbchucks feel amazing, fighting and parkour is fantastic! But missions tend to bug and now i am soft locked in the fight with bepbop, cus in his mission where you first fight him, you are supposed to go down a flight of stairs and i think something is supposed to happen… BUT the fight doesn’t start, bepbop just stands there and doesn’t even tal, literally nothing happens and no way for me to control the game… idk how this was even sought over.

Update: after the patch, the game is amazing! Love the rest of the missions up until the port… like bepbop, it soft locks when you have to fix the generator, but it doesn’t seem to when you play with friends (which I don’t have any) so once again… i’m soft locked.

The game is overall fun and despite being sensitive to motion sickness I can move without teleportation, which is great. Climbing buildings is fun and jumping from rooftops is as easy as running around. The upgrades I've encountered so far are meaningful and finding blueprints and collectables is fun. It makes you want to explore the city.

On the downside, the turtles' lair needs more interactions and actual eating animations could be fun. The game sorely needs a setting for playing sitting down vs standing up. It seems like it's supposed to auto detect, but is very wonky. I've been locked in crouching due to playing sitting down and other times it's fine while sitting in the same position. Playing standing up is fine but can become uncomfortable.

As it is now it also lacks diversity as far as repeatable events. Outside of the "strongholds" and the few random events spawning here and there, the maps are empty. While it makes exploration to find collectibles easy, it also makes the game feel less immersive.

All in all it's a good experience decently priced and the co-op is a welcome touch, but it can use some improvement.


EDIT: I recently dived back into the Batman Arkham VR game. I bring this up because the comparison keeps coming up. I could barely play it for 20 minutes before missing this game. Some people complain about the battle system not being deep enough, and true it's simple, but it's fun! Being able to jump freely, climb buildings and basically do parkour across the rooftops is, again, fun!

My point about the sitting auto-detection seems to have been addressed as I have not found myself stuck crouching in a long time.

I don't normally change my reviews, but you guys just won another star. This game is really meant to be enjoyed with others. While it's perfectly satisfactory solo, there's just something about your goofball friend talking about kpop as Mickey while stalking the Foot from the rooftops. Or about playing hide and seek in the rafters of the lair while waiting for another player. This isn't the deepest game I've played, but it's immersive, fun and surprisingly open.

The only thing keeping it from a five star is the lack of end game content. I wish finishing the story would unlock something. Maybe another character, or more upgrade slots for the turtle you finished as. Just something extra. That's all. That's my only wish now. Play with a friend or two and this will be the best fun you can have in a VR beat them up.

reviews.review_title: It’s good but could have been great.
reviews.review_description: At first I loved this game but then you realize the limits of what they made and how repetitive it can get the more time you spend in Empire City. I hope they listen to the fans and make the game great but I’m thankful to have what we do. Even with what it is, with other people it’s worth the full price.

terrific game! I was expecting just a cool arcade like the classics, got a full blown Arkham City with the turtles! The studio delivered some black magic on the platform! very satisfying climbing and traversal and intuitive and fun free combat... legit good TMNT game in VR!

developer_response.body: We're extremely flattered by the comparison, given that we're a much smaller studio. Thank you so much for taking the time to review. Booyakasha! 🍕

reviews.review_title: Type of Game to buy a VR for
reviews.review_description:
For real, bought my VR nearly 3 months ago...Batman Arkham Shadows is the only game that compares and tbh...this game is now beginning to edge that for me which should say a lot!

**DEVS PLEASE CONSIDER ADDING MULTIPLAYER WHERE WE CAN MATCH WITH RANDOMS IN SOME CAPACITY THAT DOESNT REQUIRE HAVING FRIENDS...**

developer_response.body:
That is such a flattering comparison, thank you! 🙏 We have heard the feedback for more open matchmaking and we're looking into it. In the meantime, we have an LFG dedicated channel on our Discord. Feel free to stop by! 👉 https://discord.com/invite/cortopia

Appreciate you taking the time—see you back in action 🍕

reviews.review_title: Great game for TMNT lovers! But one con.
reviews.review_description: This game is great, you play as 4 turtles, very easy to grind, very simple and immersive combat, good cartoony/comical graphics, runs smoothly, goes by the lore of TMNT, great character models, colorful areas, (Lair, Chinatown, etc) And very good gameplay with a option of co-op. But, that one con, is the OKAY community. Some are nice, some are rude, some spam racial slurs for the entire time you’re playing together, there was this one time that someone was being a psycho. ￼My Discord username is Donny for my favorite turtle. So I was obviously Donny. There was a 7-9 year old kid that joined and said, ugh I knew you would be him, can I be Donny? I said no. And he ran in circles for a full 7 minutes screaming, “I WANT DONNY I WANT DONNY I WANT DONNY!!!” And I was laughing so hard I kicked him from the server.

The game starts off rather slowly, but once you’ve defeated the first boss, things really start to pick up steam. It features fantastic multiplayer—though it only truly becomes a blast once you’ve leveled up your Turtle and equipped them with the appropriate perks. The game really gains momentum later on, and the action becomes increasingly intense. It boasts great combat, puzzles, levels, and—especially—boss battles. Furthermore, it looks excellent, and the music further enhances the immense fun factor. I didn't particularly enjoy the single-player experience, however.
Unfortunately, the final boss (who changes depending on the choices made during the game) is once again boringly designed, and the ending is very abrupt. That is a real shame. There isn't really much left to do afterwards, either, since you can essentially only keep the crime rate low. Nevertheless, we had a lot of fun.

I'm so very impressed with this game. When I seen there would be cartoon like graphics I was reluctant but seeing it in person, wow. They did a fantastic job. The fighting mechanics feel great, the side quests are superb and the turtle lair is perfection. If anyone has a link to the discord that'd be great ❤️.

developer_response.body: Here's a link to our Discord! 👉 https://discord.com/invite/cortopia Thanks for the kind words and stay radical 🐢

reviews.review_description:
The highlight for me was using the numbchucks and the parkour. The bosses were quite good as well. I really liked when you fight the robot and the mini game that comes with that was quite clever.

Most of the game is a beat em up where you just go to a place and beat up bad guys.

Not the most amazing game I have ever played but not had for the price.

I loved the game at first but it gets old real quick. The combat is beyond simple. There’s no difference between any of the turtles… simply flail your arms all around and you’ll kick butt without taking damage.  Also gets real stale fighting the same exact baddie over and over. There’s no difference whatsoever between any of the foot soldiers. They’re IDENTICAL to one another.  Add in a world completely devoid of people/npcs, and you realize there’s literally 1 character model used for 95% of the game. Real lazy.  Too bad cuz this game has so much potential. I mean seriously, a few NPC’s littered throughout the city can’t be THAT difficult to add.  The climbing and parkour is pretty good.  Ughhhh I hate leaving a bad review for turtles… one of my all time favorite cartoons/games growing up. 

Right now this game feels like it’s 50% complete. Like, they’ve got the mechanics in place, the maps built, etc, but that’s IT.  Feels like the devs are at the “now let’s get to the meat and potatoes” part of development, but decided to say “scr*w it, let’s just release 6 months early”.


UPDATE 5/11/26
I’ve had a slight change of mind… I’ve gone back a few times to play since my review and found the game to be infinitely more fun in smaller doses, like 30-45 min sessions.  I appreciate the devs response to my review and also appreciate their professionalism. I’m increasing the score to 4/5. The game DOES have a charm to it. But I gotta ask, why no TMNT theme song? Hearing that each time I loaded in would pump me up big time lol. Guessing it’s a licensing thing. Anyways, I fully recommend you buy this. It’s reasonably priced and very fun in smaller doses doses. Buy it

developer_response.body: Thanks for jumping into Empire City and for sharing your thoughts! As an indie team working within the constraints of standalone VR hardware, we had to make many choices about scope and performance while maintaining our ambition to deliver a TMNT experience on Quest that is true to the franchise.  We really appreciate longtime TMNT fans like you taking the time to weigh in 💚🐢

reviews.review_description: This game is a solid foundation to build on. Yes, it feels a bit empty and repetitive for now, but think about how empty games like Dungeons of Eternity or Ancient Dungeon were compared to what they are now. Games like those grew so large because we supported them. Let's do that here. Imagine what future updates could bring us! New levels, enemies, and items, etc. Play as Casey Jones, maybe? Or even The Shredder? I'm with you, Devs!

developer_response.body: Glad to hear you’re enjoying your time in Empire City 🐢 The game has been designed to be expandable, and you have some great ideas right here! Let's see what the future can hold :)

reviews.review_description:
This game goes so hard. i could honestly stand around in the turtle lair for hours just looking at little details and messing around with weapons. don’t even get me started on perfecting my rooftop parkour skills! i can’t wait to find all the little hidden collectibles around the city.  and i really like the constant battle with crime that really makes you feel like a superhero always fighting back relentless evil. it reminds me of the Playstation Spider-man games. 

a few bugs here and there and maybe a bit lacking n substance but it’s brand new and i’d wager they’re making an update as i type this.  overall though, a must-have for a true TMNT fan. 

                                        🐢 💚 🍕

reviews.review_title: Why there always gotta be a 1 star perso
reviews.review_description: Man innovation is key and for what it’s worth this game is fire we came a long way in gaming!!!!! And not for nothing I can’t wait to play!!!! Let’s stop hating it’s not easy to develop games to satisfy every nook and cranny lets just have fun for once geesh!!! Great work guys just get some updates in there and make it even better!!! I’m definitely here for the shenanigans!!!

Listen...no hate but the games kinda bland. I think the story's fun and the gameplay is cool but the boss fights seem kind of...quirky? It's not a terrible game, just could use some improvements. Please notice this and take it into account and make a new update please.

UPDATE: I am appreciating and enjoying this gameplay more and more as I continue the story. Still AMAZING, but I just have to request to please allow us to turn off the main mission location UI which is constantly visible, even when free roaming. UI in vr always manages to break immersion and this is a very immersive game.      
   ORIGINAL: I am not even a big fan of TMNT but this is really well made and super fun. Even you have no interest in the franchise, it's a crisp looking, open world, cartoony ninjitsu game. Don't expect B&S style combat and you will not be disappointed. The fighting here is much like the arcade game feels. You either are role playing as a ninja turtle or you are just button mashing depending on how you play. A good release for a Meta game. Nice work, devs!

It use to be fun, but there's a long list of crappy playability,that red machine in the upgrade room seems to eat the items that I've been crafting, I'll craft ,for example, 20 shurikins and when I take one away, it takes all of them in one grab, but only gives me one, must be something that needs fixing 😉

developer_response.body: Hey there! We're happy you're enjoying the game. Thank's for calling out the crafting machine struggles, it's been reported to the team to take a look at! Cowabunga! 🐢

reviews.review_description:
i agree with some of the lower rating reviewers; dude... devs need to realize the amout of X'ers playing video games ( ESPECIALLY VR) you hype up an ip like TMNT which has ALOT of nastalgia for 80'/90's kids, which has been playing there whole lives for opportunities like this and its kinda a let down!

real review:

#1. SHORT. 2 days (not 24/7). you know we used to play the arcade version (obviously what you going after) and would play for weeks on end whether in an arcade OR on NES and would still play to this day CAUSE IT WAS FUN! i beat this and WILL NEVER want to play again, Why?  cause its SHORT

#2. Focused on too much of too little.
just because everyone loved the side scoll of the arcade dont mean you just copy that format in an VR outlook.  the ENTIRE game is just jump, kick jump, kick jump, kick, yea on the OG arcade AWESOME! in a semi-open world ......uhhhhhh no 

#3. i can only assume the bulk of the $ that was put in to this game was TM's like voices, images, and bland story, IM SORRY that dont make a good game.

FINAL: its ok dont get me wrong i really did like the game...a little it just could have been soooooo much more,  you guys really let me down. theres just soo much more that could have been done to make it REMEMORABLE.  

p.s. i was soo happy to see the arcade machine in the hideout.........until i realized.....nope....just a graphic....ya'll really are cheap, the playable OG acrcade game on that cabinet would bumped you up ALOT!

reviews.review_title: Stick with it past the slow start!
reviews.review_description:
TMNT is THE IP for me, so I came in nervous. The opening hour is rough. The combat is too simple, and you can swing your way through fights without thinking. But by the halfway mark something clicks. Combat tightens, traversal becomes joyful, and roof-to-roof parkour as a Ninja Turtle is the best version of that fantasy I've played in VR. Each turtle's weapon feels distinct, with Donnie's bo and Mikey's nunchucks especially well done. 

The story nails the cartoon tone. Visually fantastic, rock-solid performance, even in four-player co-op. The free-roaming city is the biggest missed opportunity (every emergency call ends in the same skirmish, no real life on the streets), but the campaign more than carries it. 

Co-op with all four turtles is the real magic. Stick with it past the slow start.

For the full review, search YouTube for 6DOF Reviews Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles Empire City!

developer_response.body: Thank you for the 6DOF review and for posting here! Cowabunga! 🍕

reviews.review_title: It’s like playing a tmnt comic
reviews.review_description: Parkouring round the city, getting collectibles and most importantly stealing the weapons of your friends back makes tmnt so much fun, great story great combat and great traversal this game is a must buy, 10/10

I waited for Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles: Empire City like a child waiting for a miracle. I’m 42 years old, and TMNT was a huge part of my childhood. I grew up with the SNES and Sega Genesis games, the 80s cartoon, the sewers, the Technodrome, and the atmosphere of real Ninja Turtles adventures. I even pre-ordered this game because I dreamed of finally getting a great TMNT VR experience on Oculus Quest 3.

Instead, I got a shallow and disappointing game.

The turtles barely feel unique. Most of the gameplay feels more like Spider-Man than TMNT — climbing walls and rooftops nonstop, except without webs. Their signature weapons feel unfinished and lack impact.

The combat system is terrible. Hits constantly pass through enemies, and the hit detection is broken. You can punch enemies directly and nothing happens unless you hit some random “correct” point on their model, while you still take damage yourself. VR combat should feel immersive and physical, but this feels frustrating and unfinished.

The levels are empty, repetitive, and lifeless. Most of the time is spent wandering through boring environments with almost no NPCs or meaningful interaction. Where are the sewers, the Technodrome, the iconic New York streets, and the variety that made TMNT memorable?

The story is chaotic and confusing. Sometimes it’s not even clear how to interact with characters or where the plot is going.

The DLC was another disappointment. I expected classic 80s characters or meaningful content, but instead got a few lazy turtle recolors.

This game feels like a huge marketing campaign built around a primitive project sold as a “Quest 3 exclusive.” For me, it’s one of the biggest VR disappointments in years.

The saddest part is that projects like this damage the childhood memories of longtime TMNT fans and disrespect the spirit of the original franchise. This isn’t a love letter to TMNT — it’s just nostalgia being exploited for money.

developer_response.body: Thanks for taking the time to share such detailed feedback. We can clearly see how much TMNT means to you, and while Empire City takes its own approach within a very broad and long-running franchise, we’re genuinely sorry it didn’t match the version of TMNT you were hoping for. We’re a fairly small team with big ambitions for this project, and we still truly appreciate you giving the game a chance 💚🐢

reviews.review_title: Pretty fun. Has a sprint issue
reviews.review_description:
Just a classic vr beat em up. Not much to complain about. The environments are fun to traverse. The combat is pretty basic but i dont mind it. Not every vr game needs a convoluted combat system. Sometimes i just want to smack stuff.

My only real complaint is the sprinting. Its fixed to toggle(you turn sprinting on and off) Instead of normal sprinting(pressing sprint while holding the stick forward, then reverting to walking when you let go of the stick) I could definitely give this a 5 if the sprinting is adressed.

reviews.review_description:
As a lifelong fan of TMNT this was a lot of fun to play.

Exploring the city with an intuitive and expressive movement system was great. Learning the different play styles and upgrading each of the boys was also a load of fun.

Watch our Full review on 6dofreviews . com

7.5/10 - Turtely Awesome

I had a fantastic time with TMNT: Empire City. It pretty much felt as cool as I expected it to feel when I grabbed Donnie's bo off of my back for the first time. 😆 I wasn't familiar with the main bad guy from the game, so it was also neat learning some of the lore I never knew about. I would love to see what the devs can do with a follow-up if they get the chance. 🤞

I have been a Ninja Turtles fan for years, so I've been excited about this game coming out on VR! I am currently halfway through it and am enjoying it. The developers do a good job of capturing the essence of the Turtles in specific and generalised iterations. Now, in terms of the actual game...
First off the graphics are amazing! Cel-shading works best for platforms like the Quest, but how they have implemented this game really stands out! Especially the characger models. They look cartoon like but also look real. I can't explain it. The city looks really cool and sleek too!
Now people might complain that the city is empty, but they work this in narratively but also people expect too much out of a device that is pretty much a phone on your face. These sort of things we can't be elitist about if we want VR to flourish. The reason I am able to experience VR games is the affordability of the Quest. And if we want more progress and more from games then we need to understand the breakthroughs of the limitations that we currently have. The compromise for the quality in this game is needing to scale back extra bits.
And actually, in flat games I have become exhausted with massive full open worlds, so it's a welcome change of pace for things to be scaled back and focused.
The game does try to add emergent activities to fill up the space. This comes with Far Cry style outpost destroying, which have varying mini games after defeating the enemies, and also unlocking time trials and reducing the city's crime. It also comes in the form of a mixture of tasks like stopping various crimes and delivering packages. These are quite simple and become repetitive but they are also short enough to be a nice little distraction. They're also funny and the humour throughout the game is well written and voice acted!
The main trouble with these side tasks is that they appear a little too frequently. Sometimes that takes your focus away from something else you're trying to do, so if they scaled the emergent tasks back a bit then it would be more refreshing when they do happen. And also, the time trials can be fun but some of them are overly difficult. And the bomb dodging trials are too long for getting 3 stars. You need to dodge for 3 minutes, and that is especially taxing when the trial seems to not be sure whether a minute is 60 seconds or an outrageous 100 seconds. You also don't seem to get any reward from these trials, which doesn't incentivise them. But I guess also that doesn't create pressure for them either.
I will say there are a lot of warehouse missions, but they are still varied enough when inside.
The combat works well. It can get a bit scrappy, but if you focus on playing how the game hopes you will, then it works and is fun and engaging. There's a nice effort to vary the combat for each Turtle but it is quite similar and quite pointless to give different ways tl hold the weapons. But still cool. I'm terrible with the shurikens, but I do think that's also because they have a bit of an awkward soft autolock on them that hinders and helps at the same time.
There are some interesting collectibles, especially the chess pieces. It's fun to shoot some hoops and the lair is really homely. The upgrade system has some decent aspects but also some redundant ones.
The story is good and the missions work well enough. Though playing in singleplayer I am quite hampered at times by the co-op focus of the game. This focus is both the game's main strength and setback, I feel. When you die, there isn't really a drawback because you can just respawn right there in a couple seconds and get straight back into the fight. This works well in co-op but is a bit deflating in singleplayer. Plus the checkpoint systems are a bit confusing. I had to answer my phone part was through a mission so had to put the headset on standby, then i realised I had to reload the game and it started me way back. So after that I was worried to stop in fear of losing progress, yet the missions flowed in to each other and I had done too much until I realised how the checkpoints worked.
Though it is cool choosing between the Turtles and feeling who would suit each mission the best.
Oh yeah, an annoying thing is that cleared outposts get reset too quickly as well.

I know I've got some criticisms for the game and I'm blabbering on so you might not see this, but I am really enjoying it! For any criticisms I have, there are plenty of great things to counter them! The traversal is exhilarating! Climbing is awesome and a great workout! The story, the writing, the characters, the voice acting, all awesome! Oh yeah, the 90s style jazz infused boom bap hip-hop instrumental soundtrack is subtly incredible! The combat is messy, but when it works is really fun! The Turtles could have used a bit more variety to them but that's hard to do. And the feel of their weapons is really cool! Even though it's meant for co-op i am enjoying it a bunch in singleplayer! we need more games like this! We need to support quality so that it can improve. And we need tl support human innovation over AI. Peace out

Absolutely love this game its everything I wanted and hoped for but please buff these bosses or at least give them heavy stun resistance of some kind 2 ppl can easily beat bebop or rocksteady in seconds if you stun the bosses right especially with Mike or donny for the normal ninja its not as big a deal as they are alway in groups but the bosses just cant keep up please make these bosses the challenge they deserve to be

Final thoughts
Cowabunga out of 10

reviews.review_description:
It’s NOT the greatest VR game ever made. It also DOESN’T need to be.

After playing for a little while, I was fully indulging my old man childhood nostalgia and loving every second of it.

It my opinion that the devs weren’t trying to create the greatest VR game of all time, but instead were trying to create an experience that was a love letter to Gen-Xers and Millennials that let us shamelessly indulge our childhood memories. To *those* ends, I think the game is a 5/5 stars

On the whole, I’m cool with rating TMNT as a 4/5 stars

developer_response.body: We're so happy to hear you're enjoying Empire City! Cortopia is a bite-sized studio, and you are absolutely correct: we wanted to make a game for people who grew up with the franchise as we did, one that would give you the opportunity to embody a Turtle for the first time and immerse yourself in Turtledome. Cowabunga 🍕🐢

reviews.review_title: Great game! Very immersive. Great combat
reviews.review_description: This is one of the greatest games that I've played on Quest yet. The mechanics are wonderful. The parkour is very good. The nunchucks are absolutely outstanding. I think the only bad thing about the game is that it is kind of small but it is on the quest

Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles: Empire City is a love letter to TMNT fans—a fast, fluid, and frantic VR romp that nails the essence of the franchise while adding modern, open-world freedom. Choose your turtle (each with unique abilities), dive into New York’s sewers-turned-lobby, and unleash shell-shocking chaos on the city’s vermin infestation.
The execution is flawless—combat is satisfyingly weighty, missions are varied and replayable, and the progression system keeps you hooked. The atmosphere is spot-on, from the gritty streets to the neon-lit rooftops, making you feel like you’ve stepped straight into the ’80s cartoon (but with VR immersion).
But the real magic? Multiplayer. Team up with friends, compete for high scores, and clean up the city in 4-player co-op mayhem. Whether you’re a lifelong fan or a VR newcomer, this game is a must-play. Cowabunga, indeed—10/10!

developer_response.body: Thanks for playing and taking the time to review. It means the world to us! Keep it shell-tight out there 👊🐢🍕

reviews.review_description: I wish they can add like join randomly so we can make new friends

This game was so much better than I initially anticipated.

In stark contrast with many VR games, here are the BEST innovative features it brings to the table:

1. Replayability
Once you've finished the story, you can free roam the city, explore, and kick Foot ninja butt. Doesn't mean because the story is finished, you must start a new game.

2. Turtle Lair 
You actually have a base now! After a long night out in the city you'll eventually run out of gadgets, sure you can resupply during some specific events but I love going back to the lair, crafting upgrades, crafting items, switching turtles and then go out on patrol again!

If you are a TMNT fan, you'll LOVE this game.

If you enjoy action games again, you'll like it in my honest opinion

developer_response.body: Hey and thank you for dropping us a review! We'd like some more information on the Bo as we've not seen the issue pop up before, would you mind hopping on our Discord with a report so we can take a look? 👉 https://discord.com/invite/cortopia

reviews.review_description:
This game is essentially what I would compare to the Spider-Man game on PlayStation five.

There’s a clear love of the series, tons of references, you can make out all the passion in the dialogue and everything that you don’t quite see directly. This thing is filled with collectibles- you even have a good incentive for finding them!

But the main issues for the game is that it was clearly built for multiplayer in mind, the combat is really repetitive and the physics don’t really play well with it- like, for example if you wanted to play as Raphael the hit box on your said are on the side…. Meaning that the instinctive stabbing motion will do absolutely nothing but get you hurt.

It doesn’t help that certain bosses are so large that more often than not you end up hurt by accidentally walking into them, even though you have little to no ranged options other than what you can make with the games crafting system. 

And while that system is very cool , After a full playthrough I realized that I ended up having less than half of all of the blueprints you could get- because just like the collectible’s, they are also hidden.

The story also seems to have a element of choice in it, but the final encounter doesn’t seem to have been impacted by it in anyway. I won’t spoil it by any means, but you are usually given good and bad options a t least three points in the game, I chose the good option two of the three times and it seems that either you are defaulted into the same conclusion, regardless of the choice- or the one bad choice ended up ruining my run completely. it doesn’t help that you can’t really go back in the story mode at this time without abandoning your save file. I think that with some polish and maybe a few quality of life updates the game could be absolutely amazing but as it stands now, I find it to be worth the cost, but relatively disappointing ￼

developer_response.body: First of all, thank you for taking the time to review! A few of the things mentioned here, especially regarding the choices, we can clarify on our Discord to keep it spoiler-free here. We're also looking into getting better support for item backtracking. Again, appreciate you taking the time—see you back in action 🍕 Link to our Discord 👉 https://discord.com/invite/cortopia

reviews.review_description:
Really enjoying the game! The gameplay is easy to catch and fun. The collecting, upgrades, parkour are great unexpected touches. Weapons are pretty good with plenty of room for growth which is great! 

What I'd LOVE to see.. additional power moves/ combos - turtle  specific, an endless foot soldier mode where these could be unlocked, A splinter mode or storyline, progressive crime missions that unlock other boss fights from the series. 

Thanks for building with f-mod also! That is a huge bonus that could push this game into a different stratosphere! Let's go older devs and modders! Let's see if you can or a few more greys in my beard. 🤣

developer_response.body: Glad to hear you’re enjoying your time in Empire City 🐢 Thank you for taking the time to review, it means a lot to the team!

reviews.review_description:
This game is incredible! It's got some bugs and combat gets a little crazy and weird with the range for block and parry. But once you get comfortable with using dash to dodge, dash in, hit and dash out, you can handle most enemies well enough. The parkour is unbelievable! Once I learned how to stick landings on the sides of buildings I really feel like a ninja turtle trapping all over town. I can scale a building in seconds, scout enemy camps and silently take out enemies one at a time.

There are lots of secret areas and hidden collectibles like games used to have back when they were fun, before micro transactions became the standard. 

It feels a little weird that there are no other people anywhere, but overall, this is what I want from a TMNT game. So much fun, I love it!

developer_response.body: Grateful for the support—catch you back in the action 👊🍕

reviews.review_title: Best game with TELLATE series colors
reviews.review_description: Just gotta be one THAT I HAVE EVER PLAYED IN A LONG TIME STORY WISE COMICAL CONNECTIONS AND ALSO OVERALL THE COMBAT GIVES ME A FOR HONOR, KIND OF FEEL. BUT ALSO JUST OVERALL PLAYING WITH YOUR FRIENDS BEING ABLE TO PLAY AS THE TURTLES IS THE BEST THING TO EVER HAPPEN AND IF I NEED TO ADD ON, EVERYONE’S GONNA SAY THE GAME IS BORING THAT’S BECAUSE THEY NEVER PLAYED A GAME LIKE THIS ONE FACTS!!! 😂💯

developer_response.body: Thanks for playing! Keep it cowabunga out there 🐢🍕
